page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.3xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 12 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 24.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results position this instance in the average performance tier across most standardized tests. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1437.18 and a multi-core score of 11491.81. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 640.1 GB/sec, which drops to 71.29 GB/sec for large block uncached reads, and a write bandwidth of 35.57 GB/sec. In application-specific benchmarks, the instance demonstrates a static web serving throughput of 10.1 GB/sec and processes 11.24 million Redis SET operations per second.

The instance exhibits specific performance tradeoffs, particularly in memory-intensive and large-scale computational tasks. While it handles small-model LLM inference efficiently, processing 741.27 tokens per second for a 135M model, its performance drops to a weak tier of 17.31 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it suitable for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, web serving, and database operations, but less optimal for memory-heavy applications or large-scale AI inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.3xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated Intel Xeon 8269CY physical cores running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 24.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark data places the instance in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6420.0 and a static web serving throughput of 10.1 GB/sec. It processes 11.24 million Redis SET operations per second but exhibits weak performance during medium-model LLM prompt processing. This hardware profile is suited for compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and database operations where a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio is sufficient.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.3xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 24 GB RAM, and a 3.1 GHz clock speed, suited for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfc6.3xlarge features 12 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 24 GB RAM, optimized for compute-bound workloads and web serving.
tagline: A compute-optimized instance featuring twelve dedicated Intel Xeon cores and a balanced resource profile for web serving and compilation.

24.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio.
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage.
Average performance in software compilation and static web serving.
Suited for database operations, web serving, and compute-bound tasks.
